evidence_checked.command: git rev-list --count --since='7 days ago' HEAD across skillos, flywheel, mobile-eats, zeststream-platform, zesttube, clutterfreespaces, terratitle, agent-bench, cubcloud-aaas
evidence_checked.result: sample count 3755 commits; does not independently prove the 4000+ claim because the exact nine-product-surface set was not established

canonical_gates.finding: Runway is correctly modeled as a hard gate, but no runway receipt with 18+ months before sub #1 or 24+ months before sub #4 was located.

mobile-eats has strong product/substrate evidence, but no named signed owner-operator plus first paying-customer registry receipt was located. It should not be counted as a formed portfolio company under the goal's vanity-metric forbid until that registry row exists.
The 5 documented prospect-interview gate is locked in the plan; no per-candidate five-interview receipt was located.

A machine-checkable voice gate exists, but public/content surfaces in zeststream-v2-fresh still contain older automation/custom-app/workflow-builder framing. The holding-company anti-pitch rule is not fleet-green yet.
The 60h/week Year 2 ceiling and 50%+ substrate time-offset invariant are locked, but no time-offset or weekly-hours measurement receipt was located.
Warm-network-only sourcing for subs #1-2 is locked. No warm candidate list, outreach receipt, or signed owner receipt was located.

The substrate is real: mobile-eats has tenant routing, a baseline substrate-share receipt with 27 total @zeststream/* dependencies, and SkillOS records real pack adoption across three consumer repos. The missing part is a formed owner/company registry row.
Legal house scaffold exists and names the correct layers; attorney/CPA call and binding operating-agreement artifacts are still gates before sub #2 signs.
Reusable substrate and adoption receipts exist, but there is no per-launch cost/time curve proving PEEL or PRESS N+1 is cheaper than N.

recent_progress_claims.claim: 4000+ commits in 7 days across 9 product surfaces
recent_progress_claims.finding: A quick count over nine obvious local repos produced 3755 commits. This may exclude the intended ALPS/Blackfoot or worker surfaces, but the audit did not prove the exact 4000+ claim.
Share-ready packet, tenant declaration, and @zeststream/* dependencies are present. First paying-customer and signed owner-operator registry receipts were not located.

recent_progress_claims.claim: anthropic-sdk-python adoption OK across three consumer repos
recent_progress_claims.finding: canonical-gates-status records package-adoption-velocity OK with real consumer repos agent-bench, mobile-eats, and zesttube.

The skill exists with banned words, grounding rules, and scorer gates. Its current canonical line is still the older SMB time-back positioning, so it needs a holding-company alignment update before it enforces the new anti-pitch rule.

recent_progress_claims.claim: SkillOS Forever-OS v3 ratified and structure locked
recent_progress_claims.finding: The v3 goal file and session arc receipts exist under Desktop goals and SkillOS state.

next_actions.action: Create a portfolio-company registry schema that refuses to count a company without signed owner-operator and first paying-customer receipt paths.
Produce a redacted runway receipt proving the 18-month sub #1 launch gate or mark launch blocked.
Update ZestStream voice config to explicitly reject workflow-builder/custom-app-builder public framing and encode the holding-company anti-pitch story.
Inventory and grade public surfaces against the mission-alignment goal; route D/F surfaces to update plans.
If mobile-eats is intended as portfolio company #1, add owner-operator, equity, and first paying-customer receipts; otherwise keep it classified as product/substrate proof, not a counted company.
Add N+1 cost instrumentation: per-launch PEEL interviews, PRESS build hours, reused package count, new substrate contributions, and downstream propagation window.
